name: GASTRIC VOLVULUS
definition: abnormal torsion (twisting) of stomach, usually > 180° → complete obstruction → ischemia

demographics.Peak age: 40-50 years (in 20% in infants < 1 year of age); most often in elderly with hiatal
demographics.Associated with: diaphragmatic defect (hiatal hernia, diaphragmatic eventration / rupture

dominant_finding: antrum moves anterosuperiorly + fundus rotates posteroinferiorly:
discriminating_features: ingested contrast may pass through stomach
stomach becomes dilated + fills with fluid
oral contrast is retained in stomach
antrum moves anterosuperiorly + fundus rotates posteroinferiorly:

general: ingested contrast may pass through stomach
stomach becomes dilated + fills with fluid
oral contrast is retained in stomach
CXR: massively distended stomach in LUQ extending into chest /retrocardiac location (=
unexpected location of stomach gas bubble
air-fluid levels in mediastinum / upper abdomen
incomplete / absent entrance of barium into stomach
horizontally oriented stomach with a single air-fluid level:
"mirror-image" stomach = greater curvature to right of lesser curvature (in vertically
CT: antrum moves anterosuperiorly + fundus rotates posteroinferiorly:
"upside-down" stomach = lesser curvature below greater curvature
"right-side up" stomach = antrum / pylorus rotates anterosuperiorly and from right

clinical.Rx: open / laparoscopic gastropexy + repair of diaphragmatic hernia
